text: This data publication describes NeuroVault, a web site for storing, sharing, visualizing, and analyzing brain mapping data as statistical maps of the brain. NeuroVault employs modern web technologies to give an easy-to-use interface presenting detailed visualizations of the data in the browser without the need for downloading or installing local application software. A large collection of brain maps is also associated with NeuroVault, such as those from group studies found in the Neurosynth database, which are used for the cognitive decoding of uploaded maps. In addition, all of the data on NeuroVault are available via a public REST API, so other web services and applications can use the site as a database. NeuroVault is relevant to any neuroscientist making comparisons of activation, performing meta-analyses, or investigating coactivation patterns.
